You said:

how do i create a folder

ChatGPT said:

Creating a folder depends on what device or editor you’re using. Since you’re building a website, you’ll most likely use Windows + VS Code. I’ll break both down.

METHOD 1 — CREATE FOLDER ON WINDOWS

Step 1 — Go to Desktop

Open your desktop or any location you want.

Step 2 — Right-click

Right-click empty space.

You’ll see a menu.

Step 3 — Click:

New → Folder

Step 4 — Name the folder

Type:

ogun-site

Press:

Enter

Now you have your main project folder.

CREATE THE INNER FOLDERS

Open the ogun-site folder.

Then repeat the same process.

Create:

css
js
images

Your structure becomes:

ogun-site
│
├── css
├── js
└── images

CREATE HTML FILES

Inside ogun-site:

Right-click → New → Text Document

Rename them:

index.html
about.html
departments.html
churches.html
contact.html

IMPORTANT:

Make sure it changes from:

index.html.txt

TO:

index.html

If you still see .txt, Windows is hiding extensions.

SHOW FILE EXTENSIONS (VERY IMPORTANT)

Open any folder.

At the top click:

View → Show → File name extensions

Now you can properly rename files.

CREATE CSS FILE

Open the css folder.

Create:

style.css

CREATE JAVASCRIPT FILE

Open the js folder.

Create:

main.js

FINAL STRUCTURE

Your project should now look like:

ogun-site
│
├── index.html
├── about.html
├── departments.html
├── churches.html
├── contact.html
│
├── css
│   └── style.css
│
├── js
│   └── main.js
│
└── images

METHOD 2 — CREATE FOLDERS INSIDE VS CODE (BEST METHOD)

If you’re using Visual Studio Code:

Step 1 — Open VS Code

Step 2 — Click “Open Folder”

Choose or create:

ogun-site

Step 3 — Use Explorer Panel

On the left side:

Right-click your project name.

Step 4 — Click “New Folder”

Create:

css
js
images

Step 5 — Create Files

Right-click project → New File

Create:

index.html
about.html
departments.html
churches.html
contact.html

Then inside css:

style.css

Inside js:

main.js

QUICK UNDERSTANDING

Folder = container

Like a bag holding files.

Example:

images/

holds all pictures.

File = actual content

Example:

index.html

contains webpage code.

WHAT TO DO NEXT

After creating everything:

  1. Open index.html

  2. Add basic HTML

  3. Link CSS

  4. Start building page by page

Example starter:

HTML
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <title>Ogun Conference</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/style.css">
</head>

<body>
  <h1>Welcome to Ogun Conference</h1>
</body>
</html>

You said:

here CREATE CSS FILE Open the css folder. Create: style.css is it a new folder i will create inside css folder

ChatGPT said:

No — style.css is not a folder.

It is a file.

Inside the css folder, you create a file named style.css.

UNDERSTAND THE DIFFERENCE

Folder

Folders hold files.

Example:

css
js
images

These are folders.

File

Files contain code/content.

Examples:

style.css
main.js
index.html

These are files.

WHAT YOUR STRUCTURE SHOULD LOOK LIKE

ogun-site
│
├── css
│   └── style.css

This means:

  • css → folder

  • style.css → file INSIDE the folder

HOW TO CREATE style.css

If using Windows File Explorer

Step 1

Open the css folder.

Step 2

Right-click empty space.

Click:

New → Text Document

Step 3

Rename it from:

New Text Document.txt

To:

style.css

VERY IMPORTANT

Make sure it does NOT become:

style.css.txt

If it does, turn on file extensions:

View → Show → File name extensions

IF USING VS CODE (EASIER)

Inside Visual Studio Code:

  1. Right-click css

  2. Click:

    New File

  3. Type:

    style.css

Done.

SAME THING FOR JAVASCRIPT

Inside js folder:

Create file:

main.js

NOT a folder.

SIMPLE MEMORY TRICK

Folders usually have NO extension

Example:

css
images
js

Files HAVE extensions

Example:

style.css
main.js
index.html

The extension tells the computer what type of file it is.


You said:

can you help me do all these?

ChatGPT said:

Yes — I can guide you through the ENTIRE process step by step until your website is fully organized and live.

Here’s how we’ll do it together:

STEP-BY-STEP PLAN

We’ll build your project in this order:

PHASE 1 — Project Setup

We create:

  • folders

  • files

  • structure

PHASE 2 — Homepage Setup

We:

  • create index.html

  • connect CSS

  • test the website

PHASE 3 — Navigation

We:

  • create all pages

  • connect links properly

PHASE 4 — Styling

We:

  • move CSS into style.css

  • clean your design

PHASE 5 — Contact Form

We:

  • connect Formspree

  • make messages work

PHASE 6 — Deployment

We:

  • upload to Netlify

  • make your site live

LET’S START NOW

STEP 1 — CREATE MAIN F


You said:

go ahead

ChatGPT said:

Great. We’ll do this like a real developer setup.

STEP 1 — CREATE THE MAIN PROJECT FOLDER

Create a folder named:

ogun-site

IF YOU ARE USING WINDOWS

Do this:

1. Go to Desktop

2. Right-click empty space

Choose:

New → Folder

3. Name it

ogun-site

Press Enter.

STEP 2 — OPEN THE FOLDER

Double-click:

ogun-site

Now it should be empty.

STEP 3 — CREATE THESE FOLDERS INSIDE IT

Inside ogun-site, create 3 folders:

css
js
images

YOUR STRUCTURE SHOULD NOW LOOK LIKE:

ogun-site
│
├── css
├── js
└── images

STEP 4 — CREATE HTML FILES

Inside ogun-site create these files:

index.html
about.html
departments.html
churches.html
contact.html

HOW TO CREATE FILES

Inside the folder:

Right-click → New → Text Document

Rename each one properly.

Example:

index.html

NOT:

index.html.txt

VERY IMPORTANT

If you see .txt at the end:

Turn on extensions.

At the top of File Explorer:

View → Show → File name extensions

Then rename again.

STEP 5 — CREATE CSS FILE

Open the css folder.

Create file:

style.css

STEP 6 — CREATE JAVASCRIPT FILE

Open the js folder.

Create:

main.js

FINAL STRUCTURE SHOULD LOOK LIKE THIS

ogun-site
│
├── index.html
├── about.html
├── departments.html
├── churches.html
├── contact.html
│
├── css
│   └── style.css
│
├── js
│   └── main.js
│
└── images

STEP 7 — OPEN IN VS CODE

If you already installed Visual Studio Code:

Open VS Code

Then:

File → Open Folder

Choose:

ogun-site

STEP 8 — ADD YOUR FIRST HTML CODE

Open:

index.html

Paste this:

HTML
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">

<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">

  <title>Ogun Conference</title>

  <link rel="stylesheet" href="css/style.css">
</head>

<body>

  <h1>Welcome to Ogun Conference Website</h1>

</body>
</html>

STEP 9 — ADD CSS

Open:

css/style.css

Paste this:

CSS
body {
  font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
  background-color: #f5f5f5;
  padding: 40px;
}

h1 {
  color: green;
}

STEP 10 — TEST THE WEBSITE

Go back to:

index.html

Double-click it.

It should open in browser showing:

“Welcome to Ogun Conference Website”

in green color.

IF IT WORKS

Then:

✅ your HTML works
✅ your CSS works
✅ folder structure is correct
✅ project is connected properly

That means you’ve officially started building a real website.
